common.checksSummary: {0} checks evaluated · {1} passed, {2} failed, {3} warnings, {4} skipped

maturity.heading: Security Maturity
maturity.anchorNote: The lowest unmet control anchors the rating (CMMI-style scale: 1 Initial to 5 Optimized), so a single critical exposure caps the score until it is resolved.

ioe.heading: Indicators of Exposure
ioe.note: {0} open exposure(s), ranked by severity and blast radius.
ioe.more: + {0} more exposure(s) in the detailed findings below.

attackpath.heading: Attack Paths to Tier-0
attackpath.noneRan: No escalation paths to Tier-0 were found in the collected ACL scope. Deep low-privilege chains require full-domain ACL collection · re-run with -FullDomainAcl to widen coverage.
attackpath.noneNotRun: Attack-path analysis was not run. Enable the ACLDelegation + PrivilegedAccounts categories (or All), and add -FullDomainAcl for deep transitive chains.
attackpath.summary: {0} escalation path(s) reaching Tier-0 · {1} from NON-privileged principals (shown first, highest risk). Each arrow is a control or membership edge an attacker can traverse.

cartography.heading: Attack-Path Cartography
cartography.legend: Visual map of escalation routes to Tier-0. ⚑ Red = non-privileged start, orange = already-privileged, ★ blue = Tier-0 objective. Follow the arrows left to right.

comparison.heading: What Changed Since Last Run
comparison.firstRun:
This is the first recorded run at this scope. Run history begins with this
version's unified comparison engine, so earlier delta files are not read;
comparison begins with your next assessment, when this run becomes the
baseline.
comparison.versionSkew: (this run: {0}; check catalogs differ, so NEW and RETIRED entries below are expected)
comparison.lostVisibility: Lost visibility (assessed before, Not Assessed now)
comparison.stillNotAssessed: Still not assessed (dark in this run and the previous one)
comparison.newChecks: New checks in this run (not a transition)
comparison.retiredChecks: Retired checks (present before, absent now; not a transition)
comparison.noChange: No verdict changed since the previous run. {0} checks are unchanged.{1}

filter.empty: No findings match the current filter.

ad.title: Active Directory Report
ad.verdict90: The Active Directory environment demonstrates strong security posture with minimal findings.
ad.verdict75: The AD environment has good security posture with some areas requiring attention.
ad.verdict60: The AD environment has fair security posture. Several important findings require remediation.
ad.verdict40: The AD environment has poor security posture. Multiple critical and high-severity findings need immediate attention.
ad.verdict0: The AD environment has critical security deficiencies. Immediate remediation is required to prevent compromise.
ad.bloodhoundExport: An OpenGraph export of the collected AD attack graph was written to {0}.
ad.bloodhoundImport: Import in BloodHound CE: Administration → File Ingest → upload the file, then run the built-in pathfinding queries. Nodes are SID-keyed (they overlay native SharpHound data) and edges use native BloodHound kinds.

entra.title: Entra ID / M365 Report
entra.postureDesc: Entra ID / Azure / M365 security posture score (0-100)

gws.title: Google Workspace Report
gws.verdictCrit: Immediate action required. {0} critical-severity configuration failure(s) detected that expose the tenant to significant risk.
gws.verdictHigh: Remediation recommended. {0} high-severity finding(s) identified that should be addressed promptly.
gws.verdictMed: Monitor and improve. {0} medium-severity finding(s) warrant review and hardening.
gws.verdictLow: Minor gaps detected. {0} low-severity finding(s) present. Overall posture is sound.
gws.verdictPass: All checks passed. The tenant configuration meets baseline security expectations.

campaign.title: Campaign Report
campaign.scoreDesc: Campaign score (0-100) · weighted assessment of {0} checks across {1} platforms

technical.title: Technical Security Assessment Report
technical.noFindings: No audit findings available. Run a scan first.

exec.title: Executive Security Summary
exec.verdictNoScore: No score could be computed from the available findings. Run a scan to establish a baseline.
exec.verdict75: Your organization has a strong security foundation. Continue monitoring and address remaining gaps.
exec.verdict50: Your security posture has room for improvement. Priority action on critical findings is recommended.
exec.verdict0: Immediate attention required. Critical security gaps put your organization at elevated risk.
exec.maturityOverall: Overall maturity: Level {1} of 5: {2}. The lowest unmet control anchors the rating, so a single critical exposure caps the score until it is resolved (CMMI-style scale: 1 Initial to 5 Optimized).
exec.complianceIntro: The following compliance frameworks have identified gaps:
exec.quickWinsIntro: These actions can be completed at no cost using existing tools:
exec.nextStep1: Address critical findings immediately · these represent the highest risk to your organization.
exec.nextStep2: Implement the quick wins above to improve your security score with minimal effort.
exec.nextStep3: Review the detailed technical report for complete remediation guidance.
exec.nextStep4: Schedule a follow-up scan in 30 days to measure improvement.
exec.disclaimer: This report provides a point-in-time security assessment for internal planning purposes. Findings should be validated and remediated according to organizational risk tolerance.

playbook.title: Remediation Playbook
playbook.subtitle: {0} actionable item(s) · Max cost: {1}
playbook.disclaimer: Review each remediation step before implementing. Test changes in a non-production environment first.

budget.title: Security Budget Justification
budget.complianceIntro: The following compliance frameworks have gaps based on current audit findings:
budget.phase1Desc: Configuration changes using existing tools · highest ROI, immediate security improvement.
budget.phase2Desc: Minor purchases or license add-ons within existing budget.
budget.phase3Desc: License upgrades or add-on products for enhanced security capabilities.
budget.phase4Desc: Major purchases or infrastructure changes for long-term security posture improvement.
budget.phase4Body: {0} item(s) identified requiring significant investment. Contact your security advisor for detailed scoping.
budget.disclaimer: This report is for internal planning purposes.
